title: Rising trade tensions push metals lower
section_path: Rising trade tensions push metals lower
section_path_text: Rising trade tensions push metals lower
summary:
Main
Renewed trade uncertainty pushed industrial commodities lower. Signs of tightness in the oil market offset concerns of higher output from OPEC. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-07-08 115304.png | assets/2025-07-08_rising-trade-tensions-push-metals-lower_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_e6a79d32b0f5.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices rose as the market shrugged off OPEC's decision to accelerate production hikes.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Renewed trade uncertainty pushed industrial commodities lower. Signs of tightness in the oil market offset concerns of higher output from OPEC.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices rose as the market shrugged off OPEC's decision to accelerate production hikes. Eight members of the OPEC+ alliance agreed to raise output in August by 548kb/d.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: China continues to ramp up its purchases of nickel metal. China's imports of refined nickel has reached 78.7kt in the first five months of 2025, up 121% y/y.
